pass_criteria: Reviewer and sign-off principal are distinct (four-eyes).
Every revoked or modified grant has a corresponding ticket and the change was applied within 5 business days.
100% of sampled access grants are reconciled against current HR role assignments.
rationale: Quarterly four-eyes privileged access review per ISO/IEC 27001 A.5.18, SOC 2 CC6.3 and PCI DSS 4.0.1 Req. 7.2.5. n=25 gives high assurance on a population of <500 privileged grants.

pass_criteria: Every production change has a recorded peer review approval distinct from the author.
Emergency changes have a post-change review completed within 5 business days.
No production change in the period was deployed without an approval recorded in the change-management system.
rationale: Monthly inspection of change-approval logs supports ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.32 and SOC 2 CC8.1. Inspection (rather than reperformance) is appropriate because the system-of-record itself is the evidence.

pass_criteria: A full restore of the production database from backup completes successfully into an isolated environment.
Restored data integrity matches the source within the documented RPO.
Restore completes within the documented RTO.
rationale: Annual restore reperformance per ISO/IEC 27001 A.8.13, SOC 2 A1.2 and DORA Art. 12. Reperformance is the only method that proves the backup is actually restorable.
